research_question: What factors are most correlated with nonprofit success, funding growth, volunteer retention, and community impact?
findings: Median Income is strongly positively correlated with Education Pct (r = 1.00, p = 0.0000).
Retention Rate is strongly positively correlated with Engagement Score (r = 1.00, p = 0.0000).
Funding is strongly positively correlated with Volunteer Hours (r = 1.00, p = 0.0000).
Funding is strongly positively correlated with Revenue (r = 1.00, p = 0.0000).
Revenue is strongly positively correlated with Volunteer Hours (r = 0.99, p = 0.0000).
Median Income is strongly negatively correlated with Unemployment Rate (r = -0.97, p = 0.0000).
Education Pct is strongly negatively correlated with Unemployment Rate (r = -0.97, p = 0.0000).
Unemployment Rate is strongly positively correlated with Population (r = 0.97, p = 0.0000).
Higher educational attainment is associated with improved volunteer retention. Observed correlation: r = 0.96 (~96% explained variance).
Retention Rate is strongly positively correlated with Median Income (r = 0.95, p = 0.0000).
